name: Cholera sensor (CAI-1 + CRISPRa)
shortDescription: Detects Vibrio cholerae by sensing its species-specific quorum signal CAI-1. The CqsS receptor gates a CRISPRa circuit driving a fluorescent reporter, in safe E. coli K-12 — no live pathogen required.

input.detects: The Vibrio cholerae-specific quorum autoinducer CAI-1 in water samples
input.operatingRange: CqsS-based sensors respond from nM CAI-1

sensing.sensorMechanism: The CqsS histidine kinase detects CAI-1 and, through the LuxO/HapR-type relay, switches its target promoter on.
sensing.signalFlow: CAI-1 -> CqsS signalling relay activates target promoter -> transcribes an sgRNA -> dCas9-activator amplifies a fluorescent reporter (CRISPRa) -> fluorescence rises when V. cholerae signal is present.

parts.name: CqsS receptor + relay
parts.notes: CAI-1-responsive sensor kinase; requires porting the relay into E. coli.

parts.name: Reporter-activating sgRNA
parts.source: designed for CRISPRa upstream of a weak reporter promoter

output.result: Fluorescence indicates V. cholerae quorum signal in water.

performance.limitOfDetection: CqsS CAI-1 sensing: nM range (module-validated in Vibrio).
performance.notes: Senses the pathogen's signal molecule, not the live organism; the CqsS relay and CRISPRa are validated separately and integration is a design template requiring relay porting into E. coli.

safety.biocontainment: Safe E. coli K-12 reads a secreted small molecule; no BSL-2 Vibrio culture needed.
safety.notes: Cholera surveillance without handling the live pathogen — a key safety advantage of signal-molecule sensing.

buildSteps.title: Port relay + design sgRNA
buildSteps.description: Reconstitute the CqsS->LuxO->HapR relay in E. coli; design the CRISPRa sgRNA.

buildSteps.title: Assemble units
buildSteps.description: TU1: CqsS relay. TU2: quorum promoter -> sgRNA. TU3: dCas9-omega. TU4: weak promoter -> sfGFP.

buildSteps.title: Add sample
buildSteps.description: Incubate with the water sample + CAI-1 standard curve.

provenance.source: Design template combining the Vibrio cholerae CqsS/CAI-1 quorum module with E. coli CRISPRa
partsSources: Higgins et al. 2007, Nature (CAI-1 / CqsS quorum sensing)
provenance.license: Parts per their original sources; design template CC BY 4.0
